questions.q: Paragraph: $\mathrm{Pb}\left(\mathrm{IO}_3\right)_2$ is a sparingly soluble salt $\left(\mathrm{K}_{\text {sp }}=2.7 \times 10^{-13}\right)$. To $35 \mathrm{ml} 0.15 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{Pb}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2$ solution, 15 ml of 0.8 M $\mathrm{KIO}_3$ solution is added and a precipitate of $\mathrm{Pb}\left(\mathrm{IO}_3\right)_2$ is formed.Question: What will the molarity of $\mathrm{Pb}^{+2}$ ions in the solution after completion of the reaction

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: $\mathrm{Pb}\left(\mathrm{IO}_3\right)_2$ is a sparingly soluble salt $\left(\mathrm{K}_{\text {sp }}=2.7 \times 10^{-13}\right)$. To $35 \mathrm{ml} 0.15 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{Pb}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2$ solution, 15 ml of 0.8 M $\mathrm{KIO}_3$ solution is added and a precipitate of $\mathrm{Pb}\left(\mathrm{IO}_3\right)_2$ is formed.Question: Which is a limiting reagent of the reaction that takes place in the solution?
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & 1 \mathrm{~mol} \text { of } \mathrm{Pb}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right) \text { reacts with } 2 \mathrm{~mol} \text { of } \mathrm{KIO}_3 \\ & 5.25 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~mol} \text { of } \mathrm{Pb}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2 \text { reacts }=2 \times 5.25 \\ & =10.5 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~mol} \\ & \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~mol} \text { of } \mathrm{KIO}_3 \text { left }=12-10.5 \\ & =1.5 \\ & \text { Hence } \mathrm{Pb}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2 \text { is the limiting reagent }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A solution is 0.1 M in each $\mathrm{KCl}, \mathrm{KBr}$ and $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{CrO}_4$. To this solution solid $\mathrm{AgNO}_3$ is gradually added. Assume no change in the volume of the solution. $\begin{aligned}& \text { Given } \mathrm{K}_{\text {sp }}(\mathrm{AgCl})=1.7 \times 10^{-10} \\& \mathrm{~K}_{\text {sp }}(\mathrm{AgBr})=5 \times 10^{-13} \\& \text { and }_{\mathrm{spp}}\left(\mathrm{Ag}_2 \mathrm{CrO}_4\right)=1.9 \times 10^{-12}\end{aligned}$Question:The sequence of ions precipitated follows the order?
questions.solution:
$\left[\mathrm{Ag}^{+}\right]$to start the ppt. of $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$
$=\frac{\mathrm{K}_{\text {sp }}(\mathrm{AgCl})}{\left[\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right]}=\frac{1.7 \times 10^{-10}}{0.1}=1.7 \times 10^{-9} \mathrm{M}$
$\left[\mathrm{Ag}^{+}\right]$ to start the ppt. of $\mathrm{Br}^{-}$
$=\frac{\mathrm{K}_{\text {sp }}(\mathrm{AgBr})}{\left[\mathrm{Br}^{-}\right]}=\frac{5 \times 10^{-13}}{0.1}=5 \times 10^{-12} \mathrm{M}$
$\left[\mathrm{Ag}^{+}\right]$to start the ppt. of $\mathrm{CrO}_4^{2-}$
$=\left[\frac{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{sp}}\left(\mathrm{Ag}_2 \mathrm{CrO}_4\right)}{\left[\mathrm{CrO}_4^{2-}\right]}\right]^{1 / 2}=\left[\frac{1.9 \times 10^{-12}}{0.1}\right]^{1 / 2}=4.36 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{M}$
Order of ppt. $\mathrm{Br}^{-}, \mathrm{Cl}^{-}, \mathrm{CrO}_4^{2-}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Buffer is a solution whose pH does not change significantly on addition of a small amount of acid or alkali or on dilution. Answer the following questions:Question: The amount of $\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ to be added to 500 ml of $0.01 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ solution (pKa for $\mathrm{NH}_4{ }^{+}$is 9.26 ) prepare a buffer of pH 8.26 is :
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{pH}=\mathrm{pKa}+\log \frac{[\text { Base }]}{[\text { Salt }]} \\ & {[\text { Base }]=\frac{0.01 \times 500}{500}=0.01}\end{aligned}$
Let 'a' milli moles of $\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$
$\left[\mathrm{NH}_4^{+}\right]=\frac{\mathrm{a} \times 2}{500} ; \quad \because[$ Salt $]=\left[\mathrm{NH}_4^{+}\right]$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{pH}=9.26+\log \left[\frac{0.01}{2 \mathrm{a} / 500}\right] \\ & 8.26=9.26+\log \left[\frac{0.01 \times 500}{2 \mathrm{a}}\right] \\ & \mathrm{a}=25\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ Moles of $\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ added $=0.025$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Buffer is a solution whose pH does not change significantly on addition of a small amount of acid or alkali or on dilution. Answer the following questions:Question: The ratio of pH (approximately) of solution (I) containing 1 mole of $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COONa}$ and 1 mole of HCl and solution (II) containing 1 mole of $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COONa}$ and 1 mole of acetic acid in one litre is :
questions.solution: $\therefore\left[\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOH}\right]=\frac{\text { No. of moles }}{\text { Volume of solution }}=\frac{1}{1}=1$ $\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]=\mathrm{C} \propto=\mathrm{C} \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}}{\mathrm{C}}}=\sqrt{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \mathrm{C}}=\sqrt{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}}$ i.e. $\mathrm{pH}_1=-\frac{1}{2} \log \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{a}}$ Solution (II) $\rightarrow \underset{1}{\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COONa}}+\underset{1}{\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOH}}$ It is buffer. $\therefore \mathrm{pH}_2=\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{a}}+\log \left[\frac{\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COONa}}{\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOH}}\right]$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{pH}_2=\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{a}}+\log \frac{1}{1} \\ & \mathrm{pH}_2=\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{a}} \\ & \therefore \frac{\mathrm{pH}_1}{\mathrm{pH}_2}=\frac{1}{2}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Buffer is a solution whose pH does not change significantly on addition of a small amount of acid or alkali or on dilution. Answer the following questions:Question: A weak acid HA after treatment with 12 ml of 0.1 M strong base BOH has $\mathrm{pH}=5$. At the end point, the volume of same base required is $26.6 \mathrm{ml} . \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}$ of acid is : [Given $: \log \left(\frac{1.2}{1.46}\right)=-0.085$ ]
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{lclc} & \mathrm{HA}+\mathrm{BOH} & \rightleftharpoons & \mathrm{BA}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ \mathrm{t}=0 & \mathrm{a} \quad 12 \times 0.1 & 0 & 0 \\ \mathrm{t}=\mathrm{eq}(\mathrm{a}-1.2) & 0 & 1.2 & 1.2\end{array}$
Meq of $\mathrm{HA}=$ Meq of BOH at end point
$A=26.6 \times 0.1=2.66$
$\therefore$ Meq of HA left $=2.66-1.2$
Now, $\mathrm{pH}=\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{a}}+\log \frac{[\text { Salt }]}{[\text { Acid }] \text { left }}$
$\begin{aligned} & 5=-\log \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}+\log \frac{1.2}{(2.66-1.2)} \\ & \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}=8.219 \times 10^{-6}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
